state.text: ║  8×8 PIXEL SCREEN  —  direct row-select  +  SR latch per pixel        ║
║  1. Flip a ROW switch   R0 = top row  …  R7 = bottom row               ║
║  2. Set D0-D7         switch UP = pixel ON   switch DOWN = pixel OFF   ║
║  3. Hold WRITE button   release when row is latched in               ║
║  4. Repeat for every row you want to change                          ║
║  HOW EACH PIXEL CELL WORKS  (SR latch):                              ║
║   AND(row,D)  → AND(↑,WRITE) = setGate  → turns pixel ON             ║
║   AND(row,!D) → AND(↑,WRITE) = rstGate  → turns pixel OFF            ║
║   NOT(rstGate) + AND(storedQ, notRst)   = hold (keeps value idle)    ║
║   OR(setGate, hold) → DELAY → LED       = the memory loop            ║
║   DELAY output feeds back → hold AND closes the latch                ║
║  TO SCALE: each row needs 1 ROW switch + COLS pixel cells            ║
║            each column needs 1 DATA switch + 1 NOT inverter          ║
║            total gates per cell: 4 AND  1 NOT  1 OR  1 DELAY  1 LED  ║
══ SCREEN ══  LEDs show the stored image
── NOT inverters ──  one per data switch
══ LOGIC ══  one row-band per screen row, one cell-block per column
Each cell block = 4 AND  +  1 NOT  +  1 OR  +  1 DELAY   (wired as SR latch)
